result:
**Goal: Add a Ferrofluid Dynamics mode** — simulate magnetic nanoparticle suspension that self-organizes into spikes, labyrinthine stripe patterns, and hexagonal lattices under user-controlled magnetic fields. Visualize the Rosensweig instability (normal-field spike formation), chain formation under uniform fields, and labyrinthine domain patterns — all driven by the interplay of magnetic, surface tension, and gravitational forces.

This fills a clear visual gap: the project has discrete Ising spins, static magnetic field lines, and MHD plasma, but nothing capturing the mesmerizing self-organization of ferrofluids — one of the most visually striking phenomena in condensed matter physics. It would feature top-down and side-view rendering, interactive field control (strength, direction, gradient), multiple presets (Rosensweig spikes, labyrinthine maze, chain columns, field-responsive art), and the characteristic hedgehog/spike aesthetic.

Want me to build it?
